categories.title: About You
categories.description: Whether you're an individual or a business, we want to understand your situation.

options.label: Just me — I want to control my own data
Me and my family / household
Professional firm (law, accounting, healthcare, financial)
Technology company or startup
Nonprofit, advocacy, or journalism organization
Other small business or organization

questions.text: How many people would use the services?

options.label: Just me
2-5 people
More than 50

questions.text: What kind of data are we talking about?

options.label: Personal stuff — photos, passwords, files, browsing history
General business data (docs, email, scheduling)
Regulated data (legal, health, financial records)
Highly sensitive (PII, attorney-client, medical records)
I'm not sure what I even have, honestly

categories.title: Where You Are Now
categories.description: No wrong answers here — we just want to understand your starting point.

questions.text: Which cloud services are part of your daily life?

options.label: Google/Apple/Microsoft everything — email, photos, docs, passwords, the works
Mostly streaming and social media — Netflix, Spotify, YouTube
A mix — some cloud services, some stuff I manage myself
As little as possible — I already try to minimize cloud dependency

questions.text: Where does your data live today?

options.label: Everything is in the cloud — Google Drive, iCloud, Dropbox, etc.
I have a server, NAS, or something running at home/office
Mix of cloud and local storage
I don't really know where all my data is

questions.text: Does anyone help you manage your tech?

options.label: No — I figure it out myself (or don't)
Yes, an IT provider, but I'm not happy with them
Yes, an IT provider, and it's working fine
We have an internal IT person or team
I have a friend/family member who helps when things break

questions.text: What bugs you most about your current setup?

options.label: I don't really own my data — it's all in someone else's cloud
Nobody knows the full picture of what's running or how
If my hard drive died tomorrow, I'd lose things I care about
I feel locked into platforms I can't easily leave
I have no idea how exposed I actually am
I'm paying too many subscriptions for stuff that should be free
Honestly, things are mostly fine

categories.title: Security & Privacy
categories.description: Don't worry — there are no wrong answers. We're figuring out where you are, not grading you.

questions.text: Have you ever had a data scare — lost files, hacked account, ransomware, anything like that?

options.label: Yes — it was a real problem (lost data, compromised accounts)
Yes — but it was minor or I caught it quickly
No, nothing like that
I honestly wouldn't know if something happened

questions.text: If your main device died right now, how much would you lose?

options.label: Nothing — I have tested backups and know exactly where everything is
Probably fine — I have backups but haven't checked them lately
I'd lose some stuff — backups exist but I'm not sure they're current
I'd lose a lot — I don't really have a backup plan

questions.text: Do you have any compliance or regulatory requirements?

options.label: Yes — specific framework (HIPAA, SOC 2, PCI-DSS, etc.)
Yes — general expectations from clients or partners
No — this is personal / no formal requirements
Not sure

questions.text: How important is it that your data stays on hardware you control?

options.label: Very important — that's why I'm here
Important — I'd prefer it but can be flexible
Nice to have, not a dealbreaker
Not important — I'm comfortable with cloud storage

categories.title: Availability & Recovery
categories.description: How much would it matter if things went down?

questions.text: If your services went offline, how long before it's a real problem?

options.label: Minutes — this is critical for work or operations
Hours — significant inconvenience but I'd survive
A day — annoying but I'd manage
Days — it's not mission-critical for me

questions.text: How much data could you afford to lose in a worst-case scenario?

options.label: None — I can't lose any of this
A few hours of data would be OK
A day's worth wouldn't kill me
I've never thought about this before

questions.text: Do you need a copy of your data in a second location (off-site backup)?

options.label: Yes — geographic redundancy is required
That sounds smart — I'd like that
No — one location is fine
Not sure what that means yet

categories.title: Budget & Hardware
categories.description: Let's figure out what you're working with.

questions.text: What hardware do you have (or would you get)?

options.label: I have a Raspberry Pi (or I'd buy one)
I have an old laptop or desktop collecting dust
I've been eyeing a mini PC
I already have a server or NAS running
I'd need to buy something — not sure what
I have no idea what hardware I'd need

questions.text: What could you see spending monthly on managed infrastructure?

options.label: Nothing — I want to do this myself for free
Under $200/month
Over $3,000/month
Help me figure out what makes sense

questions.text: What does success look like for you six months from now?

options.label: My data lives on my hardware and I know where everything is
Everything is documented, secured, and I know it works
I'm free from cloud vendor lock-in
I can demonstrate my security posture to clients or auditors
I stop worrying and things just work
I understand how all of this works — even if I hire someone later
All of the above
